name: Cincinnati Chili
recipeIngredient: 14 1/2 ounces whole tomatoes, undrained and chopped
5 cups hot cooked spaghetti (about 10 ounces uncooked pasta)
2/3 cup reduced-fat sharp cheddar cheese, finely shredded (2 2/3 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first three ingredients in a medium saucepan; cook over medium heat until meat is browned, stirring to crumble. Drain well; return meat mixture to pan.
2. Add broth and next 11 ingredients (broth through tomatoes); bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer, uncovered, for 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Discard bay leaf.
3. Spoon spaghetti onto plates; top with chili, cheese, onion, and crackers.
Yield: 5 servings (serving size: 1 cup spaghetti, 1 cup chili, 2 tablespoons cheese, 2 tablespoons onion, and 4 crackers).
Nutritional Information:
Per serving: Calories 498 (30% from fat), Protein 32.4 g, Fat 16.7 g (saturated 6.3 g), Carbohydrates 56.2 g, Fiber 5.4 g, Cholesterol 66 mg, Iron 5.7 mg, Potassium 619 mg, Calcium 210 mg.
